card_description: St. Paul (Latin (Paulus): "Small/humble") is a historical figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the biblical tradition, St. Paul is apostle who received vision on road to damascus. PKD saw parallels between Paul's Damascus vision and his own 2-3-74 experience. Appears in 11 Exegesis passages. Associated with dictionary terms: St. Paul. Context: "Dick explores the "erotic" nature of divine possession, equating the Holy Spirit with the "Yang" that impregnates the human "Yin."

linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_71.txt

linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_56.txt

linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_70.txt

reference.brief: Apostle who received vision on road to Damascus
reference.significance: PKD saw parallels between Paul's Damascus vision and his own 2-3-74 experience
